chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, this video is about hashing technique.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hashing technique is useful for searching purpose.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now before going to hashing, let us quickly look at some of the known searching techniques.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There is linear search.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this, the elements must be arranged in an array randomly, not in any order.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the time for searching is order of n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For searching any element, we start looking for the element from the left hand side of

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: an array towards the right hand side, means we will start from the beginning index.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the time is order of n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Second search method is binary search.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this, the elements must be in the ascending order, sorted order, kept in a list, kept

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: in an array.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we always look for an element in the middle of a list.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And if the element is larger, we go on right side.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If it is smaller, we go on left hand side.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time for searching is order of log n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So important thing is the arrangement of elements in a linear search can be random.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But in binary search, it must be in the sorted order.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the time complexity for linear search is order of n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And binary search improves the search method and make it faster.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And it is order of log n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But one requisite here is that the elements must be sorted.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we have to spend some extra time in sorting the elements.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Ok now, we want a search method which takes just constant time.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So from n, we have moved to log n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And from log n, we want to move on to order of 1.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the requirement which introduces hashing technique.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see hashing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In hashing technique, if a list of elements are given and an array space is given where

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we have to store the list of elements, then we store the element upon its own index.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We take the value of an element itself as the index and we store it at same index.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 8 is stored at 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And 3, it is made as index and it is stored at location 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Go on.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now when we want to search for any key element, let us say 10, then we directly go to index

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 10 and see if the element is present or not.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you want to search for key element 12, then we directly go to index 12 and see element

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is present or not.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 12 is not found.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the search time is order of 1.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Directly we have to go to that index and look for the element.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But the problem is, if I have one more element that is 50, then we have to store it at index

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 50 which may be far away.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here for storing this 50, we should have an index 50 in the array.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So lot of space is wasted in this method.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If we want to reduce the time, then we may have to waste lot of memory.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Just for few elements, we need a very large size array where most of the spaces are vacant.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: To improve this technique or to improve this basic idea, we adopt a mathematical model.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We design a mathematical model for hashing based on functions.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us see next how it is modeled using mathematically.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The list of elements what we have, we call them as key space.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the place where we have to store them, we call it as hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we say that there is some function, say h of x equal to x, which is mapping these

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: elements from key space to hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So h of 8 is 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this 8 will be stored at index 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And same way 3 is stored at 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: h of 13 is 13.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is stored at 13, index 13.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we say that h of x equal to x is a hash function which is mapping these k elements

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: from key space to hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this we take it as an ideal hash function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the same case we have seen just now.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And we have just made it in the mathematical form.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now in this, if I have a index 50, then I should have a, if I have a key element 50,

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then I should have an index 50 in an array.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I should have a very lengthy hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Again the problem is same that lot of space is wasted.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if we see the properties of functions, there are two types of functions.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: One to one function and many to one function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: These are the two type of cardinalities followed by functions.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And h of x equal to x is a one to one function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If we take any other function other than h of x equal to x, it may be many to one function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But also it depends on the keys or the hash table size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So but mostly it will be many to one function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we say that here this function is responsible for mapping the elements to their indices.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we should modify the function to efficiently utilize the space in hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will try to modify the function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here we take hash function h of x equal to x mod.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the size of hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 10 is the size of the hash table.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So now we will take a hash table size as 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As an example I am taking the size as 10 here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Size can be, we can take a hash table of any size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So hash function I am taking it as h of x equal to x mod size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the size is 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us map the elements.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 8, 8 mod 10 gives me 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 8 is stored here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3 mod 10 is 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is stored here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 13 mod 10 is also 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there is a collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is causing collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the problem is when you change the hash function then there is a possibility that

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: two or more elements are mapped at the same index.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And that is called as collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we have to resolve this collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We have to solve this problem.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There are different methods for avoiding collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For avoiding collision or for removing collision we have different methods.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is open hashing under that is chaining.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And next is closed hashing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Under that we have linear probing and quadratic probing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us look at them one by one.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First method for resolving collision is chaining.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this method when we map any key using same hash function, hash function is same.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When we map it, it will be mapped at 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will not store the element in the hash table itself but at this index we will add

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: a new chain.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And in that we will insert the element.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Chain is nothing but a link list.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3 is mapped here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will add a node to a chain and insert 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 13 is also mapped here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will add a new node and there we will insert 13.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So when there is a collision, we will insert a key element at the same index in a chain.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So all these elements are inserted into their indices inside a chain.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, when I want to search for any key element, let us say 13, then I should use hash function.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: H of 13 is 13 mod size that is 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 13 mod 10 gives me 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So go to index 3 and search for that element, key element in a chain at that particular

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: index.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there may be more than one element so we have to search that entire chain, entire

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: link list.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's how now the time complexity of hashing will not be order of 1, it will be more than

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But it will be better than log n, it will be faster than log n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us look at other methods.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us look at next method, linear probing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In this, hash function is h of x equals x mod size, same hash function I am taking.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 8 is mapped at 8.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3 is mapped at 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 13 is also mapped at same location but there is a collision so we store it at next location

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So in this method, we simply find the next free space and store the element in next free

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: space.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is this approach?

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We should give the reasoning mathematically.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So here, there is explanation.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Our actual hash function is x mod size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We define a new hash function h dash of x that is h of x plus f of i mod size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And f of i is equal to i where i takes values 0 or 1 or 2 or so on.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now when we use 13 and we have to insert 13, then we say h of 13 plus f of 0 mod 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This gives us 3 but there is a collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So next we use the same hash function for a different value of i and find f of 1 mod

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we got the answer as 4.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's how the value is stored at location 4, index 4.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we use this h dash x as a hash function and we store the element.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If there is a collision, then we take the next value of i.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Even if there is a collision again, then we take the next value of i and so on.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let us insert the rest of the elements.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 6 is stored at 6.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And 4, there is a collision.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Already some element is there.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we store it at next place.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And 10 is stored here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So when I have to search for any key element, let us say I am searching for 4, then I should

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: use this hash function h of x equal to x mod size, go to that place, 4, but the element

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is not present there.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then I should look at the next location, that is 4.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, it is found here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is found at index 5.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it means by using the hash function, I should go to the index and start looking for

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the element in the consecutive locations.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I have a key element, if I have to search for key element 24, then 24 mod 10 gives me

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: index 4.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When I go to index 4, 24 is not present.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then I should move to next location.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is not present.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next location not present.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next location is empty.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I should stop and say that 24 is not found.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I can say that for searching any key element, we should use hash function, go to that particular

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: index and start searching for the element until the element is found or empty space

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is reached.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the method for searching.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And again, the time complexity will not be order of 1.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It will be more than 1.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But it will be better than log n.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the problem in linear probing is it causes clustering of element.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: A bunch of elements will be at one single place at consecutive location.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: They will be allocated continuously.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this may cause lot of time for searching any element.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So to avoid the clustering of elements, we introduce quadratic probing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is same as linear probing where if I take the hash function, then for this also

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we define a new function h dash of x.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is h of x plus f of i mod size.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But f of i is i square.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I takes the value 0, 1, 2 and so on.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is not f of i is not equal to i.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: f of i is equal to i square.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the difference between linear probing is in linear probing, f of i is equal to i we

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: take here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But in quadratic probing, f of i is equal to i square we take.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is the difference.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now when we try to map the key element, 8 is stored here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3, same place.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 13, for i as 0, there is a collusion.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for i as 1, it will be stored at next location.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now next is 23.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So let us find out for 23.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: h of 23 plus f of 0 mod 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This gives 3.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There is a collusion.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: At location 3, there is a collusion.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then h of 23 plus f of 1 mod 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 3 plus 1 mod 10 is 4.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: At 4 also there is a collusion.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then we find 23, h of 23 plus f of 2 mod 10.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is 3 plus f of 2 is 4 mod 10 is 7.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this 23, there is a collusion here.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is stored at index 7.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now you can see that the element is kept few locations away.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It's not very close to the elements which are getting collusion with 23.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there is a gap in between the elements.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will take, this will avoid clustering of elements and improve the searching time.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hope you have understood hashing.

chunks.title: Technique - Simplified - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Thank you for watching this video.

text: Hi, this video is about hashing technique. Hashing technique is useful for searching purpose. Now before going to hashing, let us quickly look at some of the known searching techniques. There is linear search. In this, the elements must be arranged in an array randomly, not in any order. And the time for searching is order of n. For searching any element, we start looking for the element from the left hand side of an array towards the right hand side, means we will start from the beginning index. And the time is order of n. Second search method is binary search. In this, the elements must be in the ascending order, sorted order, kept in a list, kept in an array. And we always look for an element in the middle of a list. And if the element is larger, we go on right side. If it is smaller, we go on left hand side. So the time for searching is order of log n. So important thing is the arrangement of elements in a linear search can be random. But in binary search, it must be in the sorted order. And the time complexity for linear search is order of n. And binary search improves the search method and make it faster. And it is order of log n. But one requisite here is that the elements must be sorted. So we have to spend some extra time in sorting the elements. Ok now, we want a search method which takes just constant time. So from n, we have moved to log n. And from log n, we want to move on to order of 1. So this is the requirement which introduces hashing technique. So let us see hashing. In hashing technique, if a list of elements are given and an array space is given where we have to store the list of elements, then we store the element upon its own index.
